_readme: EDITING GUIDE — (1) To activate MagicEden buttons, replace every 'PLACEHOLDER' magicEdenUrl with the real URL (per line, and collectionMagicEdenUrl for the global button). Until then the buttons render as a disabled 'soon' state. (2) To add a future mint, append NFT objects with the correct 'line' and a new 'mint' number, then run scripts/build-images.py to generate their webp images. (3) Titles/descriptions are public-facing copy; 'alt' is accessibility text describing the actual image.

collection.name: 401jK NFT Collection
collection.intro: 401jK explores the cracks in the system we were told would take care of us. Through satire, history, rebellion, and defiance, each series captures a different way people wake up, opt out, and retire on their own terms. As the collection grows, so does the story, because freedom isn't one path, one look, or one finish line.

lines.name: Legends Rewritten
lines.aesthetic: Pop art · bold colour · satirical portraits
lines.description: A pop-art rebellion remixing history's icons with 401jK's satirical punchline. Each portrait transforms a cultural legend into a co-conspirator exposing the absurdity of the retirement system. Bright, bold, and brutally honest, because even the greats knew the rigged system is a joke played on us.

lines.name: Rebellion Reframed
lines.aesthetic: Classical art · dramatic historical scenes recontextualised
lines.description: A dramatic fusion of classical art and modern financial revolt. Reframes humanity's defining uprisings through 401jK's lens. Each piece exposes how little has changed: elites still write the rules, and the rest of us are expected to applaud. This is rebellion retold.

lines.name: The Jester's Tarot
lines.description: Reimagines tarot as a 401jK journey from chaos to clarity. Through defiant Jester cards, explores nonconformity, corruption, collapse, transformation, and the choice to take back your own path. Symbolic, satirical, and surprisingly on point.

nfts.title: Winston Churchill
nfts.alt: A neon, glitch-art poster of Winston Churchill holding a Tommy gun, featuring a quote about jokes.
nfts.description: Winston Churchill (1874-1965) was the British prime minister who held the line against fascism when surrender looked like the sensible option. "A joke is a very serious thing," and so is the 401jK. We meme because the people who rigged the system want you to think the fight is unserious. It isn't.

nfts.title: Thomas Paine
nfts.alt: A glitch-art portrait of Thomas Paine wearing futuristic sunglasses, with a quote about rebellion.
nfts.description: Thomas Paine (1737-1809) was the writer whose words lit the fuse on the American Revolution and made the case that common people could govern themselves. He believed rebellion becomes a duty once the system stops serving you. The 401jK picks up where he left off: the rigged financial order took everything, so rebellion is the only move.

nfts.title: Nikola Tesla
nfts.alt: A distorted, glitch-art image of Nikola Tesla with his magnifying transmitter, featuring a quote about electricity and hate.
nfts.description: Nikola Tesla (1856-1943) was the Serbian-American inventor who gave the world alternating current (the electrical system still powering civilization) and died nearly broke after being outmaneuvered by the men who profited from his genius. "If your hate could be turned into electricity, it would light up the whole world." The 401jK runs on exactly that energy: channeling the frustration of a rigged system into something that can't be switched off.

nfts.title: Ralph Waldo Emerson
nfts.alt: A neon, distorted portrait of Ralph Waldo Emerson wearing futuristic sunglasses, with a quote about cause and effect.
nfts.description: Ralph Waldo Emerson (1803-1882) was the American philosopher and poet who made self-reliance a doctrine and told a generation that their own mind was sufficient authority. "Shallow men believe in luck. Strong men believe in cause and effect." The 401jK is cause and effect: a movement built on the simple logic that when you stop trusting the system's rules, you stop being bound by them.

nfts.title: Chief Sitting Bull
nfts.alt: A vibrant portrait of Chief Sitting Bull wearing a headdress and modern sunglasses, featuring a quote about unity.
nfts.description: Chief Sitting Bull (c.1831-1890) was the Hunkpapa Lakota war chief who led his people's resistance against a government that made promises it had no intention of keeping. "As individual fingers we can easily be broken, but all together we make a mighty fist." The 401jK community is the fist. The system counted on us staying fingers.

nfts.title: Chief Joseph
nfts.alt: A red glitch-art portrait of Chief Joseph wearing modern sunglasses, featuring a quote about big names.
nfts.description: Chief Joseph (1840-1904) was the Nez Perce chief who led his people on a 1,400-mile retreat toward freedom, outmaneuvering the US Army at every turn before surrendering with the words: "I will fight no more forever." "Big name often stands on small legs." The 401jK knows the institutions built to impress us are mostly hollow. Their authority is borrowed.

nfts.title: Harriet Tubman
nfts.alt: A gold-toned glitch-art portrait of Harriet Tubman wearing modern sunglasses, with a quote about snakes.
nfts.description: Harriet Tubman (c.1822-1913) was the woman who escaped enslavement and came back (thirteen times) to lead others to freedom when she could have simply disappeared into safety. "Never wound a snake; kill it." The 401jK doesn't poke the retirement system and hope it changes. It builds the exit route. Harriet would understand.

nfts.title: Marie Curie
nfts.alt: A blue glitch-art silhouette of Marie Curie in a laboratory setting, featuring a quote about progress.
nfts.description: Marie Curie (1867-1934) was the physicist who won the Nobel Prize twice (in two different sciences) in an era that didn't think women belonged in either one, and rewrote the laws of matter while the system pretended she wasn't in the room. "I was taught that the way of progress is neither swift nor easy." The 401jK was never a shortcut. It's the long game, played by people who refuse to wait for permission.

nfts.title: Emmeline Pankhurst
nfts.alt: A purple glitch-art historical photo of Emmeline Pankhurst addressing a crowd, featuring a quote about broken windows.
nfts.description: Emmeline Pankhurst (1858-1928) was the British activist who led the suffragette movement at a time when polite protest had failed for decades, and decided that disruption was the only language the system understood. "The argument of the broken window pane is the most valuable argument in modern politics." The 401jK breaks windows. Not glass: narratives. The retirement myth has been left unbroken for too long.

nfts.title: Henry David Thoreau
nfts.alt: A green-tinted portrait of Henry David Thoreau wearing modern meme-style sunglasses against a patterned background.
nfts.description: Henry David Thoreau (1817-1862) was the American philosopher and author who walked into the woods to prove a point: that a life deliberately chosen on your own terms is worth more than any salary or title the system could offer. "I have great faith in a seed - convince me you have one, and I am prepared to expect wonders." The 401jK is a seed. Small, stubborn, and irreverent. The wonders are already in motion.

nfts.title: Liberty Leading the People
nfts.alt: A satirical edit of the painting 'Liberty Leading the People', featuring the central figure wearing meme sunglasses and holding a '401JK' flag.
nfts.description: This NFT reimagines Liberty Leading the People by Eugène Delacroix, 1830. The painting shows Liberty leading the people during the July Revolution, and now she leads the 401jK community. Same fight. New enemy: a system that makes you wait forty years for permission to retire.

nfts.title: Surrender of Lord Cornwallis
nfts.alt: An edited classical painting of military figures where the central leader wears meme sunglasses and two flags display '401JK'.
nfts.description: This NFT reimagines Surrender of Lord Cornwallis by John Trumbull, 1820. The painting shows the mighty British empire laying down its arms to a ragtag colonial army it swore could never win. Now the 401jK community is the underdog, facing down the rigged financial system. And just as then, the underdog will win against all odds.

nfts.title: The Spirit of '76
nfts.alt: A modified version of the painting 'The Spirit of '76', with the flag bearer holding a '401JK' flag.
nfts.description: This NFT reimagines The Spirit of '76 by Archibald Willard, 1876. The painting shows three battered musicians marching on through smoke, refusing to stop the beat, and now they drum for the 401jK movement. We keep marching. We keep memeing. The beat doesn't stop.

nfts.title: Washington at Trenton
nfts.alt: An edited classical portrait of George Washington wearing meme sunglasses and a large gold chain with a '401jK' pendant.
nfts.description: This NFT reimagines General George Washington at Trenton by John Trumbull, 1792. The painting shows Washington calm before a battle everyone said he'd lose, the night that turned a dying cause around, and now he stands for the 401jK. Same long odds. New impossible crossing: opting out of a rigged retirement.

nfts.title: The Storming of the Bastille
nfts.alt: An altered painting of the Storming of the Bastille, featuring revolutionaries wearing meme sunglasses and a '401JK' flag.
nfts.description: This NFT reimagines The Storming of the Bastille by Jean-Pierre Houël, 1789. The painting shows an armed Paris mob tearing down a symbol of royal tyranny on July 14, 1789, the spark that lit the French Revolution, and now the 401jK flag flies over the breach. The fortress changed. The fight didn't.

nfts.title: Reply of the Zaporozhian Cossacks
nfts.alt: A humorous edit of the painting 'Reply of the Zaporozhian Cossacks', where multiple figures wear meme sunglasses and a '401JK' flag flies in the background.
nfts.description: This NFT reimagines Reply of the Zaporozhian Cossacks by Ilya Repin, 1891. The painting shows a crowd of Cossacks laughing uproariously as they compose the most gloriously defiant letter in history to a Sultan who demanded their surrender, and now they write it to the financial system. Different empire. Same answer.

nfts.title: Kościuszko at Racławice
nfts.alt: A modified historical battle painting featuring soldiers in meme sunglasses, a '401JK' flag, and a banner reading 'OCCUPY RETIREMENT'.
nfts.description: This NFT reimagines Kościuszko at the Battle of Racławice by Jan Matejko, 1888. The painting shows Polish peasant farmers (armed with nothing but scythes) routing a professional army against every sensible prediction, led by a general who bet everything on people the system had written off. The 401jK community is the kosynierzy. The scythe is the meme.

nfts.title: Storming the Winter Palace
nfts.alt: An edited painting of a colonial-era battle where key commanders wear meme sunglasses and a flag reads '401JK'.
nfts.description: This NFT reimagines Storming the Winter Palace by Vladimir Serov, 1938. The painting shows the night the Bolsheviks took history's wheel and ended an order that everyone said was permanent, and now the 401jK flag rises above the breach. Systems built on extraction and inequality end. This one just hasn't been told yet.

nfts.title: Oath of the Thirty-Three
nfts.alt: An edited painting of soldiers celebrating around a '401JK' flag, with several figures wearing meme sunglasses.
nfts.description: This NFT reimagines El Juramento de los Treinta y Tres Orientales by Juan Manuel Blanes, 1877. The painting shows thirty-three Uruguayan patriots raising their swords in an oath to liberate their homeland against every practical calculation, and now the 401jK community takes its own oath. Not with swords. With conviction. Same fire.

nfts.title: Blessing the Flag of Revolution
nfts.alt: A modified painting of an assembly in an ornate church, where a bishop and others wear meme sunglasses and hold a '401JK' flag.
nfts.description: This NFT reimagines The Bishop of Old Patras Germanos Blesses the Flag of Revolution by Theodoros Vryzakis, 1865. The painting shows the moment the Greek War of Independence began, a bishop lifting a flag and an entire people choosing, in that instant, to fight for their freedom, and now the 401jK flag is blessed by the same defiance. Old enough to be a painting. Young enough to matter today.

nfts.title: The Fool
nfts.alt: A vibrant tarot card titled 'THE FOOL' featuring a jester wearing futuristic goggles surfing on a cosmic wave.
nfts.description: The Fool is the leap before the ledger says it's safe, the nonconformist who steps off the edge while everyone else waits for permission. Pure potential, zero apology. The rigged system calls it reckless because it can't control it. This is where the 401jK story opens: rejecting the prescribed path, trusting your own. Card Zero. Chaos, by choice.

nfts.title: The Magician
nfts.alt: A golden-toned tarot card titled 'THE MAGICIAN' showing a goggled jester holding a glowing wand over a table with mystical objects.
nfts.description: The Magician is chaos forged into power. Where the old order says wait, defer, comply, he says manifest: the tools, the skill, the will, all already in your hands. This is the 401jK thesis in a single card: individual agency over inherited limits. Stop asking. Start building.

nfts.title: The High Priestess
nfts.alt: A dark, mystical tarot card titled 'THE HIGH PRIESTESS' featuring a seated woman with a jester hat, goggles, and an all-seeing eye book.
nfts.description: The High Priestess doesn't argue. She sits between the pillars (the old world and the new) and sees through both. She holds the book not to read from it, but to remind you that the knowledge was always yours. Where the system demands data, proof, and institutional approval, she asks only that you trust what you already sense. The 401jK community recognizes her: the quiet knowing that the retirement promise was hollow before anyone showed you the numbers.

nfts.title: The Moon
nfts.alt: A surreal tarot card titled 'THE MOON' depicting two kneeling jesters vomiting glowing green liquid beneath a massive, crying moon wearing sunglasses.
nfts.description: The Moon is the system's favorite card to deal you. Uncertainty, distortion, financial noise so thick you can't see your hand in front of your face. Fear fills the gap where truth should be: quarterly earnings reports, market corrections, conflicting expert opinions, retirement projections that assume you'll live exactly long enough to run out. The 401jK is what happens when you choose to walk through the fog anyway.

nfts.title: The Jeet
nfts.alt: A dark tarot card titled 'THE JEET' showing a grotesque goblin hoarding gold coins in a flooded, gloomy urban underpass.
nfts.description: The Jeet was loyal until convenience expired. Pledged the vision, held the bag long enough to take a screenshot, then exited at the first sign of turbulence. The system trained you to be a Jeet: to shrink your horizon to the next quarterly return, to mistake panic for prudence, to celebrate survival instead of transformation. The 401jK holds. Not blindly. With conviction. Be the Fool, not the Jeet.

nfts.title: The Devil
nfts.alt: A fiery tarot card titled 'THE DEVIL' featuring a winged jester figure on a throne, surrounded by chained, bowing jesters.
nfts.description: The Devil doesn't force anything. He builds a cage comfortable enough that most people never test the lock. The system's version is more sophisticated: a salary, a mortgage, a matched contribution plan, a career progression that stretches just far enough ahead that leaving always seems unwise. The chains are golden. The key is always within reach. The 401jK is the moment you decide the comfort isn't worth the contract.

nfts.title: The Tower
nfts.alt: A cosmic tarot card titled 'THE TOWER' showing a stone tower struck by a red force, with a floating jester hat and goggles above.
nfts.description: The Tower falls without asking for your calendar. Structures built on false promise (the pension guarantee, the retirement timeline, the "just keep contributing" doctrine) don't decline gracefully. They collapse. And when they do, the Jester loses the hat, loses the goggles, loses the costume, and finds out what was underneath was enough all along. The 401jK is what you build after: on ground you chose, with materials you understand.

nfts.title: Death
nfts.alt: A dark tarot card titled 'DEATH' depicting a reaper in a jester hat and sunglasses holding a scythe and an hourglass.
nfts.description: Death in the Jester's Tarot isn't the end. It's the door you were afraid to open because the system told you there was nothing on the other side. The scythe clears what has already stopped living: the deferred dream, the forty-year contract, the "work now, live later" bargain signed on behalf of a future self who was never consulted. The 401jK community steps through. What dies is the version of you the system needed you to stay.

nfts.title: Judgement
nfts.alt: A vibrant tarot card titled 'JUDGEMENT' featuring a winged jester blowing a golden trumpet from the heavens.
nfts.description: Judgement is the moment the fog clears and you stop pretending you didn't already know. The trumpet sounds and everything comes into focus: the compound interest that compounded for someone else, the retirement age that kept moving, the freedom that was always one more year away. The 401jK is a collective Judgement: a community that heard the call and decided not to lie back down. Not blame. Not rage. Just clarity, and what you do with it.

nfts.title: Justice (Reversed)
nfts.alt: A tarot card titled 'JUSTICE' depicting an upside-down jester holding a sword and a set of scales.
nfts.description: Justice reversed doesn't mean justice failed. It means it was never set up to be applied equally. The scales were calibrated in the boardroom. The sword points where power directs it. The tax code has two architectures, the retirement system has two outcomes, and the laws read differently depending on which floor of the building you occupy. The 401jK doesn't wait for the system to correct itself. It builds a ledger that can't be tilted.

nfts.title: The Hierophant (Reversed)
nfts.alt: A dark tarot card titled 'THE HIEROPHANT' showing a papal figure with a jester hat hanging upside down.
nfts.description: The Hierophant upright is the institution's spokesperson: trust the system, follow the doctrine, defer to the structure. Reversed, he hangs upside down, and from this angle you can finally read the foundation. The pension was the sacrament. The 401(k) was the confession booth. Forty years of compliance in exchange for absolution they never intended to grant. The 401jK burns the doctrine in the street. What it builds instead answers to no cathedral.

nfts.title: Two of Fucks
nfts.alt: A psychedelic tarot card titled 'TWO OF FUCKS' showing a seated, blindfolded jester holding up both middle fingers.
nfts.description: Two of Fucks is not apathy. A complete accounting was made. The math was done carefully, the inventory taken, the calculation verified, and the answer came out to exactly zero. Not angry. Not defeated. Just done. Done performing retirement readiness. Done pretending the forty-year path leads somewhere worth walking. The 401jK community reaches this card and discovers: zero fucks given to the system is an infinite supply available for everything else.
